mechanism_narrative: The 3-month trend in retail sales is used as a continuous demand signal: rising RSAFS indicates strengthening household spending and expected revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing into a higher federal funds rate regime raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average by at least 0.25 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS tightening crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment signals weakening labor income, rising recession risk, and lower expected corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered average returns are not statistically below zero at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth has a stronger positive equity effect when payroll employment is also rising, because simultaneous gains in spending and jobs confirm durable income-driven demand and support positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports PAYEMS at 132697.0 and RSAFS at 407805.0
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive RSAFS and PAYEMS trends do not outperform periods with only one positive trend over the following 20 trading days, or if interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The 6-month trend in industrial production is used continuously as a production-cycle signal: rising INDPRO indicates expanding output and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 6-month INDPRO growth is not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with a rising federal funds rate creates an inflation-driven tightening shock that pressures margins and valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises versus its prior 6 months and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports CPIAUCSL at 227.505 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase signal has nonnegative average 20-day returns, or if the interaction term fails to add predictive power versus either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A material downside crossing in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations, or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A short-term inflation surge raises near-term fears of tighter policy and margin compression, causing investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL upside crossings are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations, or if average triggered 5-day returns are not below zero.
